title: Tools beyond the 12 required capabilities
evidence: lib/brazilian_soccer/mcp/tools.ex defines compare_teams, biggest_wins, best_record, list_competitions in addition to the spec's required queries
suggestion: None — extra read-only tools are additive and well-tested

title: Cross-source dedup for correct aggregates
evidence: lib/brazilian_soccer/queries/source.ex:primary_per_season/1 picks one authoritative source per {competition,season} so standings/records/stats aren't double-counted across overlapping CSVs
suggestion: None — sound handling of overlapping datasets

title: token_efficiency scored 0.0 for this run
evidence: retort.db / scores.json: token_efficiency=0.0 (normalized; most tokens consumed in the cohort) while test_coverage=1.0, code_quality=1.0
suggestion: Informational only — the run is correct and complete; cost was high relative to peers, consistent with the TDD red-green-refactor cadence

title: Team-name matching is heuristic
evidence: lib/brazilian_soccer/team_name.ex normalizes via base/clean keys; inconsistent spellings across CSVs are reconciled by normalization rather than a canonical mapping
suggestion: Acceptable for this dataset; a canonical alias table would harden edge cases but is beyond spec
